jokes.text: Yeah. Well, nachos are nachos.
jokes.setup: Lisa praises Homer for being enlightened by taking them to a WNBA game
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals his shallow, food-motivated reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - deflates Lisa's idealism with brutal honesty and food obsession in minimal words

jokes.text: Whoa, check out Janeane Giraffalo.
jokes.setup: Introduction of tall basketball player
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of Janeane Garofalo and giraffe
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining celebrity reference with physical observation, though feels slightly forced

jokes.text: Szechwan, eh? No wonder she's so spicy. [GROWLS]
jokes.setup: Learning player is from Szechwan province
jokes.punchline: Homer connects cuisine spiciness to sexual attraction, complete with growl
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer food-sex connection, predictable but in character

jokes.text: Bart, you're a little young to be growling at 7-foot Chinese ladies.
jokes.setup: Bart mimicking Homer's growling behavior
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact parental correction with specific absurd details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - treating absurd situation as normal parenting concern

jokes.text: She showed little girls everywhere that they can grow up to be 6'5".
jokes.setup: Discussing Lisa Leslie as a role model
jokes.punchline: Celebrating height as an inspirational achievement rather than skill
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of shallow inspirational messaging, well-constructed

jokes.text: I think you mean I have game. Try to speak correctly. / You go, girl. / Yes, I will depart lest your bad grammar rub off on me.
jokes.setup: Fan uses slang 'you got game' to compliment Lisa Leslie
jokes.punchline: Lisa Leslie becomes increasingly pretentious about grammar
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from correction to pompous exit, though character feels generic

jokes.text: Now, here's something for the men to dribble over.
jokes.setup: Announcing mascot appearance
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre using basketball terminology
jokes.explanation: Lazy double entendre, obvious and not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, I can see her logo and everything.
jokes.setup: Homer attracted to mascot Swish
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on commercial branding during moment of attraction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer moment - sexualizing corporate branding is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, darn it. Swish was everything I'm not. Put the head back on.
jokes.setup: Mascot head comes off revealing it's a man
jokes.punchline: Homer's disappointed reaction and desperate request
jokes.explanation: Homer's pathetic attempt to maintain his fantasy is both funny and sad

jokes.text: The Cowgirls versus The She-gles. Heh.
jokes.setup: Reading about women's football teams
jokes.punchline: Female versions of Cowboys and Eagles team names
jokes.explanation: Simple gendered team name joke, predictable but serviceable

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Speed walking, ballroom dancing...rhythmic gymnastics, extreme choir.
jokes.setup: Asked if he likes male sports
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly feminine/non-traditional 'male' sports
jokes.explanation: Good escalation ending with absurd 'extreme choir' - plays on Homer's ignorance

jokes.text: Can't we just pretend you're really a girl? / Well, for how long? / For the rest of our lives.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to be female to take the shot
jokes.punchline: Homer's willingness to commit to lifelong gender deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - casual commitment to permanent deception for momentary gain

jokes.text: I'm donating this whole check to Bibles for Belgians.
jokes.setup: Ned wins $50,000 and announces donation
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and unnecessary charity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned charity - unnecessary, specific, and perfectly in character

jokes.text: I'm crazy as a crap-house rat for philanthropy.
jokes.setup: Owner explaining his generous donation to Ned
jokes.punchline: Folksy saying about being crazy for charity
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with colorful saying, though not groundbreaking

jokes.text: That cuts the mustard with me.
jokes.setup: Offered ride in Wienermobile
jokes.punchline: Food pun response to hot dog car
jokes.explanation: Predictable food pun but fits Ned's wholesome enthusiasm

jokes.text: Oh, it's not fair. I always wanted to drive a food-shaped car.
jokes.setup: Watching Ned get the Wienermobile
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific and absurd lifelong dream
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer - having a specific dream about something most people never consider

jokes.text: How come all the good things happen to Jesus H. Nice? By which I mean Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer jealous of Ned's luck
jokes.punchline: Calling Ned 'Jesus H. Nice' then clarifying
jokes.explanation: Good Homer jealousy moment with religious nickname for Ned

jokes.text: That's because you keep passing out on our lawn.
jokes.setup: Homer notices Ned's grass is greener
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals the real reason their lawn looks bad
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of 'grass is greener' with Homer's drinking problem

jokes.text: and flossing my teeth, tail and toes.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his success through good habits
jokes.punchline: Including impossible body parts in flossing routine
jokes.explanation: Ned's excessive hygiene taken to absurd anatomical extremes

jokes.text: They so consumed my life...I never had time for a proper haircut.
jokes.setup: Ken Burns documentary about himself discussing his passions
jokes.punchline: Explaining his distinctive bowl cut hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Smart celebrity parody explaining recognizable physical trait

jokes.text: Oh, merciful God...who has blessed mankind with two kinds of clam chowder...help me find the remote.
jokes.setup: Homer trying prayer for the first time
jokes.punchline: Elaborate theological opening for trivial request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer prayer mixing profound gratitude with laziness

jokes.text: I've never actually been to a baseball game. The fresh air gives me hives.
jokes.setup: Ken Burns famous for baseball documentaries
jokes.punchline: He's never been to a game due to absurd medical condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - the baseball documentarian can't handle outdoor sports

jokes.text: From now on, I'll pray till my hands are chapped and bleeding.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers prayer works
jokes.punchline: Commits to excessive, self-harmful devotion
jokes.explanation: Homer taking good thing to harmful extreme, very in character

jokes.text: Oh, Lord. Please guide that diaper into someone's schnoz.
jokes.setup: Watching angry monkey throw diaper at judges
jokes.punchline: Praying for slapstick violence
jokes.explanation: Homer using prayer for petty entertainment, darkly funny

jokes.text: Vengeful God, loving God. Vengeful God, loving God.
jokes.setup: Homer at work with prayer setup
jokes.punchline: Treating theological concepts like a coin flip
jokes.explanation: Homer reducing complex theology to simple binary choice

jokes.text: Whose banana you gotta peel to get a 5.9? Pfft!
jokes.setup: Discussing monkey figure skating judging
jokes.punchline: Sexual favor metaphor using monkey-appropriate fruit
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of 'who do you have to blow' to monkey context

jokes.text: I am reminded of Colonel Sanders who is now seated at your right hand...shoveling popcorn chicken into thy mouth.
jokes.setup: Homer praying and thinking of God's appearance
jokes.punchline: Imagining KFC founder as divine figure feeding God
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd religious imagery combining fast food and theology - peak Homer

jokes.text: Move over, eggs. Bacon just got a new best friend: fudge.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers fudge fell from truck
jokes.punchline: Declaring new breakfast combination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food enthusiasm with absurd combination announcement

jokes.text: Homer, God isn't some kind of holy concierge.
jokes.setup: Homer asking God for homework help
jokes.punchline: Marge's apt metaphor for Homer's prayer abuse
jokes.explanation: 'Holy concierge' is perfect description of Homer's transactional approach to prayer

jokes.text: Lord, please use your space-age clog-busting powers on this stubborn drain...then take some time off for yourself. Fly to France, have dinner.
jokes.setup: Homer praying to fix drain
jokes.punchline: Treating God like overworked employee needing vacation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of 'holy concierge' concept - Homer being considerate to deity

jokes.text: Marge, he's way the hell up there.
jokes.setup: Marge says people pray silently
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of God's location requiring volume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - distance requires volume, simple and in character

jokes.text: Your pipes have more roots than the list of all time top-rated TV shows.
jokes.setup: Explaining pipe damage from tree roots
jokes.punchline: Comparison to TV show rankings controversy
jokes.explanation: Forced metaphor connecting plumbing to media criticism, feels out of place

jokes.text: You was hiding behind the drywall. Yes, you were. I'm glad social services didn't see this. Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Plumber discovering mold behind wall
jokes.punchline: Baby-talking to mold like it's a child, referencing neglect
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating household damage as child neglect case

jokes.text: If you could fix my house...or make a new house from one of my ribs.
jokes.setup: Homer praying for help with house damage
jokes.punchline: Biblical reference to Adam and Eve creation story
jokes.explanation: Clever biblical reference showing Homer's shallow religious knowledge

jokes.text: I'm Larry H. Lawyer, Junior. And I will fight for you. I also habla Español.
jokes.setup: Ambulance chaser approaching injured Homer
jokes.punchline: Generic lawyer name and awkward Spanish phrase
jokes.explanation: Standard ambulance chaser parody, competent but not inspired

jokes.text: I couldn't help it. Those pews are so comfortable.
jokes.setup: Reference to being mocked in church bulletin
jokes.punchline: Excuse for inappropriate church behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer excuse-making, in character but not exceptional

jokes.text: He got me $60,000. And I was driving drunk in a graveyard.
jokes.setup: Client testimonial for lawyer
jokes.punchline: Reveals extremely guilty circumstances of case
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal testimonial to absurd criminal admission

jokes.text: It's his skull. It's hypnotic.
jokes.setup: Asked why he always gives in to Homer
jokes.punchline: Blames Homer's bald head for hypnotic power
jokes.explanation: Absurd explanation for Homer's influence, great visual callback

jokes.text: She was run over by a clergyman.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asking about judge's wife
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response about tragic irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of darkly ironic situation

jokes.text: Ha, ha, Your Honor, we the jury find that to be America's Funniest Home Video. Back to you, Bob Saget.
jokes.setup: Jury watching Homer's accident footage
jokes.punchline: Jury delivers TV show verdict instead of legal verdict
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke blending legal proceedings with TV show format. Works as cultural commentary on court TV.

jokes.text: Ha, ha, Your Honor, we the jury find that to be America's Funniest Home Video. Back to you, Bob Saget.
jokes.setup: Jury watching Homer's accident video
jokes.punchline: Treating courtroom as TV show taping
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about TV format confusion, solid but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: We're not a synagogue.
jokes.setup: Church claims they don't have money
jokes.punchline: Invoking stereotype about Jewish wealth
jokes.explanation: Lazy stereotype-based joke, relies on prejudice rather than wit

jokes.text: He's not giving it to me. God is because I prayed for it.
jokes.setup: Judge gives church to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer credits divine intervention for legal ruling
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic connecting lawsuit victory to prayer

jokes.text: Dad, I think this might be the work of Satan. / It's all good.
jokes.setup: Lisa warns about evil origins of church acquisition
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive modern slang response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Lisa's moral concern and Homer's casual amorality

jokes.text: Aw, he thinks he's papal.
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper wearing religious garb
jokes.punchline: Play on 'he thinks he's people' with papal reference
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on common pet behavior phrase

jokes.text: If it weren't for Alcoholics Anonymous...I'd still be sucking the juice out of glow sticks.
jokes.setup: AA testimonial about rock bottom
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and dangerous addiction behavior
jokes.explanation: Darkly creative escalation of addiction desperation

jokes.text: Saint Pauli Girl's Cathedral.
jokes.setup: Suggesting alternative meeting location
jokes.punchline: Church named after beer brand mascot
jokes.explanation: Appropriate for AA character to know beer-themed church

jokes.text: by throwing the bitchingest beer bash this church has ever seen.
jokes.setup: Homer promises to make amends for unchristian behavior
jokes.punchline: Plans even more inappropriate church activity
jokes.explanation: Homer's backwards logic making things worse while trying to help

jokes.text: Wow, Homer, this is like a party Dick Clark would throw. Yeah. Fun, but not too fun.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Homer's church party
jokes.punchline: Dick Clark as example of restrained entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference for bland, safe entertainment

jokes.text: Wow, Homer, this is like a party Dick Clark would throw. / Yeah. Fun, but not too fun.
jokes.setup: Carl comparing the party to Dick Clark
jokes.punchline: Homer agrees it's deliberately restrained fun
jokes.explanation: Good Dick Clark parody - captures his reputation for safe, corporate entertainment. The 'not too fun' line clinches it.

jokes.text: Dr. Hibbert, must you play the claw machine?
jokes.setup: Lovejoy trying to give sermon in bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Parishioner distracted by arcade game during service
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing bowling alley's inappropriate venue

jokes.text: Particularly the part about the pin in the gutter on lane 15.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Lovejoy's sermon
jokes.punchline: Mistakes bowling lane management for religious message
jokes.explanation: Good situational comedy - Marge trying to find religious meaning in mundane bowling alley announcements.

jokes.text: There's no place for me here.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy's failed attempts at bowling alley ministry
jokes.punchline: Defeated admission of ministry failure
jokes.explanation: Sad character moment showing Lovejoy's crisis of faith

jokes.text: It looks like God has packed up and left Springfield.
jokes.setup: Reverend's despair at the state of his ministry
jokes.punchline: Declares God has abandoned the town
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment - Lovejoy's theological despair expressed in practical terms. Works for the plot and character.

jokes.text: Do I have a magic lens that can see into people's souls? Well, yours would be black, Kent. Black as the ace of spades.
jokes.setup: Kent asks Arnie to film people coping spiritually
jokes.punchline: Arnie attacks Kent's character instead
jokes.explanation: Good Arnie Pie character moment - hostile and unprofessional

jokes.text: Would you believe a pigeon stuffed with Spam? A rat filled with cough drops?
jokes.setup: Trying to impress with fake gourmet cooking
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting and absurd food combinations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation with increasingly gross offerings

jokes.text: CARL: Hey, Lenny, covet some more chili fries? That's it. The whole shebang.
jokes.setup: Homer noting broken commandments
jokes.punchline: Final commandment broken with food pun on 'covet'
jokes.explanation: Clever completion of commandment-breaking with food-based coveting

jokes.text: Speaking in tongues, what great kids.
jokes.setup: Rod and Todd babbling incoherently
jokes.punchline: Ned interprets it as religious phenomenon rather than childhood nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned - interprets normal childhood behavior as religious miracle. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Ha, ha, read them and strip, Barn. Uh-uh-uh-uh. Slowly. Make me forget my troubles.
jokes.setup: Strip poker game
jokes.punchline: Moe's creepy encouragement of Barney's undressing
jokes.explanation: In-character creepiness from Moe but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yeah, and some of them's gotta be chicks. Yeah, with, like, a thousand boobs.
jokes.setup: Discussing multiple gods
jokes.punchline: Reducing female deities to sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Predictable male fantasy response, in character but unoriginal

jokes.text: On Dasher, on Dancer. Man, I must be wasted.
jokes.setup: Barney worshipping mounted deer head
jokes.punchline: Realizes his intoxication level through Christmas reference
jokes.explanation: Good Barney moment - brief moment of self-awareness

jokes.text: Thank you, God. Now, turn the rainwater into wine.
jokes.setup: Homer grateful for rain to stop fire
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for biblical miracle upgrade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer greed - never satisfied, always asking for more

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Cut that out.
jokes.setup: Ned loading animals onto ark
jokes.punchline: Having to stop animal mating despite only bringing males
jokes.explanation: Callback to Ned's no-hanky-panky rule with visual payoff

jokes.text: Yeah. I say we skin him alive and set him on fire. / Yeah, that'll appease God.
jokes.setup: Mob wanting to punish Homer
jokes.punchline: Suggests extreme violence will please God
jokes.explanation: Standard mob mentality humor. The violence is over-the-top but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: They were misled by a demon in blue pants.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy praying for town's forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Specific clothing description of Homer as demon
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific description elevating Homer to demonic status

jokes.text: I don't know. Buddha? Hey, they've suffered enough.
jokes.setup: Lisa can't explain why rain stopped
jokes.punchline: Buddha appears as deus ex machina with casual comment
jokes.explanation: Clever resolution with Buddha's casual divine intervention

jokes.text: Keep that popcorn chicken coming, colonel.
jokes.setup: Heavenly scene with God and Colonel Sanders
jokes.punchline: Callback to Homer's earlier prayer vision
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback to Homer's absurd theological vision made real
